successCriteria.description: Card enters the tracked board at the Review column (status pending) and is visible there before any forward move — validated at GET.

Card advances one column at a time through Plan -> Execute -> Shipping; each forward state.mjs transition is accepted (exit 0) and writes the expected status, exercising every intermediate kanban stage.
Card reaches the terminal Completed column with all-green pipeline / Completed styling; deriveDisplayStatus maps the final state to column 'completed' with pipelineState 'complete'.
Each status value persists to .tiki/state.json (the persistence layer) and survives a re-read; the completed card lands in history.recentIssues so a reload still renders it Completed.

No illegal transition errors during the walkthrough: forward and backward moves that ALLOWED_MOVES + the transition table permit are accepted, and an illegal pair is rejected (state.mjs exit 1) with no state corruption.

phases.title: Forward-walk verification: Review -> Plan -> Execute persisted correctly
phases.content: No code change. Validate that the pipeline transitions already emitted (GET pending, REVIEW reviewing, PLAN planning) plus the EXECUTE transition wrote the canonical status to .tiki/state.json, and that deriveDisplayStatus maps each status to the expected kanban column (pending/reviewing->review, planning->plan, executing->execute). Re-read state.json to confirm persistence (SC4). Confirm the EXECUTE transition is accepted by state.mjs (exit 0).
verification: state.json status === 'executing' after the EXECUTE transition (re-read from disk)
deriveDisplayStatus maps executing -> column 'execute' (per STATUS_TO_COLUMN in deriveDisplayStatus.ts)
all forward transitions returned exit 0 (no illegal-pair rejections)

phases.title: Bidirectional + illegal-move guard: backward legal, illegal rejected
phases.content: No code change. The acceptance criteria require exercising forward AND backward moves and graceful rejection of illegal ones. (a) Confirm the ALLOWED_MOVES adjacency in KanbanBoard.tsx permits a backward edge from execute (execute -> review). (b) Confirm the state.mjs transition table rejects an illegal pair (e.g. completed -> reviewing, or pending -> shipping) with exit 1 and leaves state.json untouched (atomic temp+rename means a rejected transition never partially writes). This validates SC5 without actually regressing the live card backward (we keep #267 moving forward to ship it clean).
verification: ALLOWED_MOVES['execute'] includes 'review' (backward edge exists in the UI layer)
an illegal state.mjs transition exits non-zero and does NOT mutate state.json (re-read shows unchanged status)
a legal same-status / forward transition still exits 0 (idempotent re-emit is a safe no-op)

phases.title: Terminal stage + persistence reload: Shipping -> Completed renders Completed
phases.content: No code change. After SHIP moves the card to status 'shipping' (Shipping column), completion removes issue:267 from activeWork and appends it to history.recentIssues + history.lastCompletedIssue (no parentRelease, so the standalone-issue completion path). Verify: (a) deriveDisplayStatus for an issue in history.recentIssues with no activeWork entry returns column 'completed', label 'Completed', pipelineState 'complete' (all-green) — this is the terminal styling SC3 asks for; (b) re-reading state.json confirms the card is durably in history (SC4 reload-survival) and absent from activeWork.
verification: after completion, issue 267 is in state.json history.recentIssues and history.lastCompletedIssue
issue:267 removed from activeWork (standalone completion path, no parentRelease)
